title: Meeting calendars and information
summary: This source is the Federal Reserve's official FOMC meeting calendar page, listing scheduled meeting dates for 2021-2027 along with links to historical statements, minutes, and press conference materials. It is a navigation/index page, not an archiveable policy document itself. No policy decisions, economic data, or substantive commentary are contained herein.
key_points: The FOMC holds eight regularly scheduled meetings per year plus additional meetings as needed.
Meeting minutes are released three weeks after each policy decision date.
Meetings marked with asterisk (*) are associated with a Summary of Economic Projections (SEP/dot plot).
The page provides links to PDF and HTML versions of all statements, minutes, and implementation notes from 2021-2027.
Future 2027 meeting dates are listed: January 26-27, March 16-17, April 27-28, June 8-9, July 27-28, September 14-15, October 26-27, December 7-8.
why_it_matters: This is a utility reference page for locating FOMC policy documents. The actual archiveable content resides in the linked statements, minutes, and projection materials—not in this calendar index itself. Researchers should navigate to specific meeting documents for policy-relevant content.
important_numbers: 8 regularly scheduled FOMC meetings per year
3 weeks after meeting for minute release

issues_found: Source is a navigation_page per source_type classification
No substantive policy content exists—only calendar dates and links to external documents
event_type set to 'unknown' which is correct since no specific FOMC event or policy content is contained herein

notes: This source is classified as a navigation_page per SOURCE-TYPE GUIDANCE. It contains no policy decisions, economic data, speeches, or research findings—only a chronological listing of FOMC meeting dates with links to documents. While it is a high-trust domain (federalreserve.gov), the content itself is structural metadata rather than substantive research material. Actual archiveable content would include the linked PDF/HTML statements, minutes, and projection materials from individual meetings.
